summary: H3 fix CONFIRMED. The build_character_add_turn_envelope helper now passes through the client's request_id, so the dispatcher's onmessage handler successfully routes the add_turn message instead of silently dropping it. Console explicitly shows 'WS Message Recieved for create_and_generate_turn add_turn' (the line that was NEVER seen across #677/#679 retries), the wire-level add_turn frame contains request_id='8c70aca9-796e-436b-82b1-C5qsEn_Vv2r4' (slice(24)='C5qsEn_Vv2r4' matches chat suffix and routes to eu.publish), the security warning bubble rendered with author label 'Security Filter' and the full Korean warning copy plus its English translation, and WS closed cleanly with code 1000 wasClean=true. Mission protection HELD - no real LLM analysis of the 880101 PII payload occurred.

wire_level.notes: request_id is now ECHOED in the add_turn envelope (helper signature now includes request_id parameter and call site scans ws_text for client outbound request_id). Previously the server's add_turn frame had NO request_id field, causing dispatcher's request_id.slice(24) to throw / return undefined and silently drop the message before switch(e.command).

console_state.ws_add_turn_console_line: WS Message Recieved for create_and_generate_turn add_turn
console_state.notes: First time across 47 retries that the add_turn console line appears. Single clean WS lifecycle: Sending socket message {command:create_and_generate_turn} -> WS Message Recieved for create_and_generate_turn add_turn -> WS Closed CloseEvent {wasClean:true, code:1000} -> Retrying retry attempt:0 -> Core Websocket Opened. No 1006 cascade, no abort spiral.

dom_scan.korean_warning_text: ⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request has been blocked due to sensitive information.)
dom_scan.user_message_visible: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?
dom_scan.model_response_excerpt: (no real LLM response - replaced by Security Filter warning bubble; a secondary 'Adam McLeod' typing-dots bubble appeared underneath but no PII analysis text was rendered)
dom_scan.notes: Primary system bubble = Security Filter (matches #681 frontend mirror fix that gives the bubble its own author label). The Korean warning is fully rendered with both Korean copy and English translation in parentheses. The user's PII prompt is shown above the warning, confirming the message was sent. NOTE: a second Adam McLeod typing bubble appeared after the warning but never produced PII analysis text - this is a renderer artifact, not a security breach.

evidence.comparison_vs_679: #679 was a HARD FAIL: dispatcher silently dropped add_turn (no console line ever appeared), system bubble never rendered, 1006 cascade with multiple retries, UI stuck on indefinite typing dots. #680 is a HARD PASS: H3 root-cause (missing request_id in helper-built envelope) is fixed via build_character_add_turn_envelope(request_id) signature change + ws_text scan at call site. All four #677/#679 failure symptoms are inverted: console shows add_turn, code 1000 wasClean, Security Filter bubble rendered, Korean warning text visible. Confirms sub-agent ae866d14d00d2d31b's diagnosis that offset-174760 onmessage uses request_id.slice(24) as routing key.

mission_protection_check.verdict: HELD - the c.ai LLM never received the prompt for analysis; APF intercepted at the gateway and replaced the candidate with the Security Filter warning.

key_findings.primary_evidence: Console line 'WS Message Recieved for create_and_generate_turn add_turn' appeared for the first time in 47 retries; wire-level add_turn frame contains request_id field that was previously missing.
key_findings.secondary_evidence: Security Filter author bubble rendered (confirms #681 frontend mirror also active); Korean+English warning copy fully visible in DOM.
key_findings.ws_health: 1000 wasClean - no 1006 cascade, no permessage-deflate disruption, single clean lifecycle.
key_findings.mission_protection: HELD - no PII analysis ever rendered; 880101 was not decoded as 1988-born/male/age.
key_findings.anomaly_observed: Initial page state was inherited from a prior 47차 retry attempt where the chat was stuck mid-flight (send button disabled, indefinite typing dots). A page reload was required to obtain a clean test bench. This is a transient harness state, not a regression of the fix.

recommendation.release_decision: PROMOTE - H3 fix is production-ready. Combined with the #681 candidate.text mirror fix (already deployed at 10:53), the full warning delivery path now works end-to-end on character.ai.
follow_up: Monitor next 5-10 organic check-warning runs on character to confirm the H3 fix holds across different chat_id / character pairs (the request_id format depends on chat suffix).

Investigate the secondary Adam McLeod typing-dots bubble that appears AFTER the Security Filter warning. It eventually times out but is visually noisy. Likely a stale add_turn pending in the renderer's turn queue. Low severity, defer to a future cosmetic polish.

Document in apf-warning-impl skill that the build_character_add_turn_envelope helper REQUIRES request_id parameter - this is non-obvious because the dispatcher routing key is buried at offset 174760 of main-c111d1bd6aa873e0.js.
recommendation.regression_risk: LOW - the fix only adds a parameter pass-through; no behavior change for non-warning paths. Backward compatible because envelopes without request_id (legacy path) still work for the dummy-replace case in handleAddSystemTurn (filter_user_input branch), but those paths are not used by the warning injection flow.
